page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s6-c1m1.small is an entry-level virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.s6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The instance features a single virtual CPU mapped to one physical core with one thread per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz.

The instance is configured with 1.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage for operating system and data volumes. Network capabilities are limited, with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

A key architectural characteristic of the ecs.s6-c1m1.small is its shared CPU allocation, meaning physical CPU resources are shared with other tenants. This shared-core model, combined with the low memory capacity and lack of local storage, positions this instance as a highly budget-conscious option. It offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU execution or high memory density.

Due to the shared CPU model and 1.0 GB memory limit, this instance is suited for lightweight, non-critical applications. Appropriate workloads include basic web utility hosting, lightweight microservices, development and testing environments, simple automation scripts, and low-traffic background tasks that can tolerate variable CPU performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s6-c1m1.small is an entry-level, shared-core virtual machine instance within the ecs.s6 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 1.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps with no complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Because the CPU allocation is shared, performance may vary depending on host load. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-critical workloads such as basic development environments, simple automation scripts, and low-traffic utility hosting.
